name: Triton (OpenAI GPU Compiler)

performance.notes: Achieves cuBLAS-level performance for custom kernels; used in Flash Attention implementation

agent_readiness.notes: Python-based GPU kernel programming language by OpenAI. Write custom GPU kernels in Python-like syntax — no CUDA C required. @triton.jit decorator. Tile-based programming model. Used to implement Flash Attention, PagedAttention, and other high-performance ML kernels. PyTorch integrates Triton for torch.compile() custom kernels. Significantly easier than CUDA C for GPU kernel development while achieving similar performance.

gotchas: GPU kernel programming knowledge still required (different paradigm than Python)
NVIDIA GPU required (AMD ROCm support experimental)
Significant learning curve for non-GPU programmers
best_when: Custom GPU kernel development without writing CUDA C
Implementing custom attention mechanisms and ML operations
Performance-critical ML kernel research and development
avoid_when: Standard ML training (PyTorch + cuDNN is sufficient)
Teams without GPU kernel programming background
summary: Triton is OpenAI's Python-based GPU kernel programming language. Write custom CUDA-level kernels using Python-like syntax without CUDA C. Used to implement Flash Attention, PagedAttention, and other state-of-the-art ML kernels. Integrated with PyTorch's torch.compile(). Significantly easier than CUDA C while achieving comparable performance. Essential for custom ML kernel research.
